title: नवरत्न स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नवरत्न स्तोत्रम् ॥

नवग्रहनवरत्नानां सम्बन्धं यो विजानति।
सर्वग्रहप्रसादेन सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ माणिक्य (सूर्य) ॥
माणिक्यं सूर्यरत्नं च रक्तवर्णं प्रभाकरम्।
सूर्यग्रहप्रसादार्थं माणिक्यं धारयेद् बुधः॥१॥

॥ मोती (चन्द्र) ॥
मुक्ता चन्द्रस्य रत्नं च शुभ्रं शीतलकान्तिमत्।
चन्द्रग्रहप्रसादार्थं मुक्ताफलं च धारयेत्॥२॥

॥ मूँगा (मंगल) ॥
प्रवालं मङ्गलरत्नं रक्तं तेजस्विसुन्दरम्।
मङ्गलग्रहशान्त्यर्थं प्रवालं धारयेद् बुधः॥३॥

॥ पन्ना (बुध) ॥
मरकतं बुधरत्नं च हरितं सुप्रभान्वितम्।
बुधग्रहप्रसादार्थं पन्नं धारयेद् बुधः॥४॥

॥ पुखराज (गुरु) ॥
पुष्यरागं गुरुरत्नं पीतवर्णं सुशोभनम्।
गुरुग्रहप्रसादार्थं पुष्यरागं च धारयेत्॥५॥

॥ हीरा (शुक्र) ॥
वज्रं शुक्रस्य रत्नं च श्वेतं दीप्तिमदुत्तमम्।
शुक्रग्रहप्रसादार्थं वज्रं धारयेद् बुधः॥६॥

॥ नीलम (शनि) ॥
नीलं शनैश्चरे रत्नं नीलवर्णं सुशोभनम्।
शनिग्रहप्रसादार्थं नीलमणिं च धारयेत्॥७॥

॥ गोमेद (राहु) ॥
गोमेदं राहुरत्नं च मधुवर्णं तपोमयम्।
राहुग्रहप्रसादार्थं गोमेदं धारयेद् बुधः॥८॥

॥ लहसुनिया (केतु) ॥
वैदूर्यं केतुरत्नं च चित्रवर्णं सुशोभनम्।
केतुग्रहप्रसादार्थं वैदूर्यं धारयेद् बुधः॥९॥

॥ सामूहिक मन्त्र ॥
माणिक्यं तरणेः सुजात्यममलं मौक्तिकमिन्दोः सवितुः
माहेयस्य च विद्रुमं मरकतं सौम्यस्य गारुत्मतम्।
देवेज्यस्य च पुष्यरागमसुराचार्यस्य वज्रं शनेः
नीलं निर्मलमन्यतश्च विमलं राहोश्च गोमेदकम्।
केतोर्वैदुर्यमिति प्रोक्तं रत्नानि शुभानि वै॥१०॥

नवरत्नधृतं यस्तु सर्वग्रहसमन्वितम्।
सर्वरोगविनाशश्च सर्वसम्पत्करं परम्॥

॥ इति श्री नवरत्न स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Navaratna Stotram ||

Navagrahanavaraatnānāṁ sambandhaṁ yo vijānāti.
Sarvagrahaprasādena sarvasiddhimavāpnuyāt.

|| Māṇikya (Sūrya) ||
Māṇikyaṁ Sūryaratnaṁ cha raktavarṇaṁ prabhākaram.
Sūryagrahaprasādārthaṁ Māṇikyaṁ dhārayed budhaḥ.||1||

|| Motī (Chandra) ||
Muktā Chandrasya ratnaṁ cha śubhraṁ śītalakāntimat.
Chandragrahaprasādārthaṁ muktāphalaṁ cha dhārayet.||2||

|| Mūṅgā (Maṅgal) ||
Pravālaṁ Maṅgalaratnaṁ raktaṁ tejasvīsundaram.
Maṅgalagrahāśāntyarthaṁ Pravālaṁ dhārayed budhaḥ.||3||

|| Pannā (Budha) ||
Marakataṁ Budharatnaṁ cha haritaṁ suprabhānvitam.
Budhagrahaprasādārthaṁ pannaṁ dhārayed budhaḥ.||4||

|| Pukharāj (Guru) ||
Puṣyarāgaṁ Gururatnaṁ pītavarṇaṁ suśobhanam.
Gurugrahaprasādārthaṁ Puṣyarāgaṁ cha dhārayet.||5||

|| Hīrā (Śukra) ||
Vajraṁ Śukrasya ratnaṁ cha śvetaṁ dīptiamaduttamam.
Śukragrahaprasādārthaṁ Vajraṁ dhārayed budhaḥ.||6||

|| Nīlam (Śani) ||
Nīlaṁ Śanaiśchare ratnaṁ nīlavarṇaṁ suśobhanam.
Śanigrahaprasādārthaṁ nīlamaṇiṁ cha dhārayet.||7||

|| Gomed (Rāhu) ||
Gomedaṁ Rāhuratnaṁ cha madhuvarṇaṁ tapomayam.
Rāhugrahaprasādārthaṁ Gomedaṁ dhārayed budhaḥ.||8||

|| Lahsuniyā (Ketu) ||
Vaidūryaṁ Keturatnaṁ cha chitravarṇaṁ suśobhanam.
Ketugrahaprasādārthaṁ Vaidūryaṁ dhārayed budhaḥ.||9||

|| Sāmūhika Mantra ||
Māṇikyaṁ taraṇeḥ sujātyamamalaṁ mauktikamindoḥ savituḥ
Māheyasya cha vidrumaṁ marakataṁ saumyasya gārutmatam.
Devējyasya cha puṣyarāgamasurāchāryasya vajraṁ śaneḥ
Nīlaṁ nirmalamanyataścha vimalaṁ rāhoścha gomedakam.
Ketorvaidūryamiti proktaṁ ratnāni śubhāni vai.||10||

|| Iti Shrī Navaratna Stotraṁ sampūrṇam ||
hindiMeaning: जो नवग्रह और नवरत्नों का सम्बन्ध जानता है, उसे सर्वग्रह प्रसाद से सर्वसिद्धि प्राप्त होती है। (1) माणिक्य (Ruby) सूर्य का रत्न है, लाल वर्ण, प्रभाकर - सूर्य ग्रह प्रसाद के लिए बुद्धिमान धारण करे। (2) मोती (Pearl) चन्द्र का रत्न है, शुभ्र, शीतल कान्ति वाला। (3) मूँगा (Coral) मंगल का रत्न है, लाल, तेजस्वी, सुन्दर। (4) पन्ना (Emerald) बुध का रत्न है, हरित, सुप्रभायुक्त। (5) पुखराज (Yellow Sapphire) गुरु का रत्न है, पीत वर्ण, सुशोभन। (6) हीरा (Diamond) शुक्र का रत्न है, श्वेत, उत्तम दीप्ति वाला। (7) नीलम (Blue Sapphire) शनि का रत्न है, नीलवर्ण, सुशोभन। (8) गोमेद (Hessonite) राहु का रत्न है, मधुवर्ण, तपोमय। (9) लहसुनिया (Cat's Eye) केतु का रत्न है, चित्रवर्ण, सुशोभन। (10) प्रसिद्ध श्लोक: माणिक्य सूर्य का, मोती चन्द्र का, मूँगा मंगल का, पन्ना बुध का, पुखराज गुरु का, हीरा शुक्र का, नीलम शनि का, गोमेद राहु का, वैदूर्य केतु का - ये नौ शुभ रत्न कहे गए हैं।
benefits: Describes the sacred connection between nine planets and nine gems
Knowledge and worship of Navaratna grants all accomplishments
Wearing appropriate gemstone pacifies respective planetary afflictions
Contains the famous Manikyam Taraneha verse describing all nine gems
Invokes health, wealth, and freedom from all planetary doshas
Complete guide to Navaratna worship in stotra form
description: Navaratna Stotram (नवरत्न स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to the Nine Planets (Navagraha). Drawn from Jyotish Shastra / Garuda Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to complete guide to Navaratna worship in stotra form as well as describe the sacred connection between nine planets and nine gems. Additionally, it is known to grant health, wealth, and freedom from all planetary doshas. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 6 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Navaratna Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Navaratna Stotram and what does it teach?
seoFaqs.answer: The Navaratna Stotram is a Sanskrit hymn that establishes the sacred correspondence between the nine planetary deities (Navagraha) of Vedic astrology and the nine principal gemstones (Navaratna) of Indian gemology. It contains a verse for each pairing — Manikya (Ruby) for Surya, Mukta (Pearl) for Chandra, Praval (Red Coral) for Mangal, Marakata (Emerald) for Budha, Pushyaraga (Yellow Sapphire) for Brihaspati, Vajra (Diamond) for Shukra, Nila (Blue Sapphire) for Shani, Gomedha (Hessonite) for Rahu, and Vaidurya (Cat's Eye / Chrysoberyl) for Ketu — and concludes with the celebrated samuhika-mantra Manikyam Taraneh Sujatyam that lists all nine in a single verse. It is the foundational scriptural text for ratna-chikitsa (gem therapy) in jyotisha. (Sources: Garuda Purana Achara Khanda Ratna-pariksha; Brihat Samhita 80-83; jyotisha gemstone manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: Which scriptural source preserves the Navaratna Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Navaratna correlation is preserved in multiple classical sources. The Garuda Purana, Achara Khanda chapters 68-80 (the Ratna-pariksha section), gives the most detailed exposition of the nine gems, their planetary lords, their varieties and their dosha-detection. Varahamihira's Brihat Samhita chapters 80-83 covers the same material in classical jyotisha. Buddhabhatta's Ratna Pariksha is a dedicated gemological treatise that systematises the planet-gem correspondences. The famous samuhika-mantra Manikyam taraneh sujatyam-amalam mauktikam-indoh savituh is a jyotisha-stotra widely circulated in stotra collections and pancharatna manuals. The text on this page synthesises these sources into a single recitation. (Sources: Garuda Purana 68-80; Brihat Samhita 80-83; Buddhabhatta Ratna Pariksha.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the nine gems and which planet does each one represent?
seoFaqs.answer: The complete Navaratna correspondence: (1) Manikya (Ruby — Padmaraga) — Surya/Sun, deep red, worn on right ring finger; (2) Mukta (Pearl — Moti) — Chandra/Moon, white-creamy lustre, worn on right small finger; (3) Praval (Red Coral — Munga) — Mangal/Mars, vermilion-orange-red, worn on right ring finger; (4) Marakata (Emerald — Panna) — Budha/Mercury, green, worn on right little finger; (5) Pushyaraga (Yellow Sapphire — Pukhraj) — Brihaspati/Jupiter, golden-yellow, worn on right index finger; (6) Vajra (Diamond — Hira) — Shukra/Venus, white sparkling, worn on right middle finger; (7) Nilamani (Blue Sapphire — Neelam) — Shani/Saturn, deep blue, worn on right middle finger; (8) Gomedha (Hessonite Garnet) — Rahu, honey-coloured, worn on right middle finger; (9) Vaidurya (Cat's Eye Chrysoberyl — Lehsunia) — Ketu, golden-grey with chatoyant streak, worn on right middle finger. (Sources: Garuda Purana Ratna-pariksha; classical Indian gemmology.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous Manikyam Taraneh Sujatyam verse?
seoFaqs.answer: The samuhika or comprehensive verse — Manikyam taraneh sujatyam-amalam mauktikam-indoh savituh / Maheyasya cha vidrumam marakatam saumyasya garutmatam / Devejyasya cha pushyaragam-asuracharyasya vajram shaneh / Nilam nirmalam-anyatash-cha vimalam rahosh-cha gomedakam / Ketor-vaidurya-miti proktam ratnani shubhani vai — encapsulates the entire Navaratna teaching in a single shloka. It is recited at the moment of ratna-dharana (gemstone wearing) and at the conclusion of Navagraha-puja. The verse names each planet by its classical epithet (Tarani for Sun, Indu for Moon, Maheya for Mars-son-of-Earth, Saumya for Mercury, Devejya for Jupiter, Asuracharya for Venus, Shanaih-shanaih for Saturn) and assigns each its sacred ratna. Memorising this verse is considered essential for any serious student of jyotisha or ratna-shastra. (Sources: Garuda Purana; widely circulated jyotisha pancharatna stotra collections.)

seoFaqs.question: How does wearing the appropriate gemstone help with planetary remedies?
seoFaqs.answer: Classical jyotisha holds that each gemstone is the crystallised solar emission of its corresponding planet — the gemstone resonates with and amplifies the planet's beneficial cosmic frequency on the wearer. When a planet is benefic in the natal chart but weak (debilitated, combust, or in a dustana house), the gemstone strengthens its positive influence. When a planet is undergoing its mahadasha or antardasha, the gemstone harmonises the wearer with that planetary period. However, gemstones must be selected carefully — the wrong gem (especially for an enemy planet of the lagna lord) can intensify negative effects. Traditional ratna-vidhi requires consulting a qualified jyotishi, choosing a flawless stone of minimum carat weight, and energising it with mantra-japa on the planet's weekday during the planet's hora before wearing. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra ratna-adhyaya; Garuda Purana Ratna-pariksha.)

seoFaqs.question: Can a person wear all nine gems together as a Navaratna ring?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes — the Navaratna ring or pendant containing all nine gems set in a specific pattern (Surya/Ruby in the centre with the other eight surrounding it) is a traditional ornament worn for comprehensive planetary harmony. The advantage of a Navaratna ornament is that no single planet is favoured over another, so there is no risk of imbalancing the natal chart by strengthening one planet at another's expense. Many traditional Indian families gift a Navaratna ring on auspicious occasions for general prosperity, health and protection. The Navaratna Stotram on this page is the recommended recitation when first wearing such an ornament, or when performing its annual abhisheka and re-energisation, ideally on Akshaya Tritiya, Diwali or one's birthday. (Sources: classical ratna-shastra; Indian jewellery traditions.)

seoFaqs.question: Is gemstone therapy effective without recitation of the Navaratna Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Classical tradition holds that gemstones must be sanctified by mantra-japa to fully activate their planetary resonance — an unconsecrated stone is considered an inert mineral rather than a living spiritual instrument. The Navaratna Stotram serves as the verbal counterpart of the physical ratna-dharana ritual: the verses invoke each planetary deity to bless the corresponding gemstone with their grace. The stotra is therefore recited (a) when first acquiring or wearing a gem, (b) annually on the planet's special day (e.g. Sunday for Ruby), (c) when the gem is re-set or re-polished, and (d) as a daily protective recitation by anyone wearing planetary gems. Recitation also benefits those who cannot afford gems by invoking the same planetary blessings through sound alone. (Sources: classical ratna-pratishthana ritual; Tantric gem-energising procedures.)
